video:video.video:title: Trump says US military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang with help from Venezuela
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday that a “swift and lethal kinetic” U.S. strike has killed Héctor Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called “the infamous leader” of the Tren de Aragua gang.

video:video.video:title: Pet owners in Gaza struggle to feed and treat their animals amid a shortage of veterinary supplies
video:video.video:description: Beyond a few facilities that treat mostly farm animals, just two veterinary clinics for common house pets remain operational across Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Carín León says family taking extra precautions after elevator scare
video:video.video:description: During an interview at the Empire State Building on June 4, 2026, Mexican singer Carín León shared an update on his wife after an elevator accident left her with an ankle injury.

video:video.video:title: Paraguay fans upset by loss to USA in World Cup group opener
video:video.video:description: Paraguay fans in Asuncion reacted with dismay to their national team's defeat against co-host USA in the opening match of their group in the World Cup tournament.

video:video.video:title: UK's Trooping the Colour flypast captured in London skies
video:video.video:description: Aircraft from Britain's Royal Air Force were captured roaring through London skies on Saturday, as the King celebrated his official birthday with the Trooping of the Colour ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds march in Kathmandu for Nepal's Pride Month
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of members of Nepal's LGBTQ+ community and their supporters took to the streets of Kathmandu on Saturday to celebrate Pride Month.

video:video.video:title: Trump's name is gone from the Kennedy Center's façade
video:video.video:description: The removal of Trump's name closes one of the more unusual chapters in the history of the Kennedy Center, which began construction in 1964 and was dedicated to the memory of the slain president, Democrat John F.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse of workers covering signage at the Kennedy Center before removing Trump's name
video:video.video:description: Timelapse of workers covering signage at the Kennedy Center before removing Trump's name, after a judge ruled it was illegally added to the building.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes strike Nabatiyeh in south Lebanon as troops advance in nearby villages
video:video.video:description: Heavy smoke was seen rising from an Israeli airstrike at the entrance of the city of Nabatiyeh. Israel's military appears to be trying to make as many gains as possible in case a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trooping of the Colour ceremony in London on King Charles III's official birthday
video:video.video:description: Fast jets soared above London's Buckingham Palace on Saturday, leaving a trail of red, white and blue smoke, in a culmination of the king's official birthday celebrations.

video:video.video:title: Collapsed tent during Virginia church's 20th anniversary celebration kills 1 and injures 22
video:video.video:description: A large tent collapsed during a Virginia church's 20th anniversary celebration on Friday evening, killing one person and injuring nearly two dozen others, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Can you speak World Cup? We quizzed fans on soccer slang
video:video.video:description: From “squeaky bum time” to “parking the bus,” soccer has its own language. We asked World Cup fans if they could define some of the sport’s most iconic phrases.

video:video.video:title: Trump's name is gone from the Kennedy Center's facade, official says
video:video.video:description: Much of President Donald Trump's name was removed from the facade of Washington's Kennedy Center overnight after a federal judge issued an order.

video:video.video:title: What we know about a possible deal to end the Iran war
video:video.video:description: The apparent breakthrough in negotiations comes after Iran exchanged fire with the U.S. and Israel over three days this week, threatening to push the region into a full-scale war.

video:video.video:title: Mayor Mamdani celebrates as New York Knicks win NBA championship
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani celebrated the Knicks win in a bar on Manhattan's Canal Street on Saturday night.

video:video.video:title: Delirious celebrations in New York as Knicks win first NBA championship in 53 years
video:video.video:description: The Knicks rallied from a 16-point deficit to beat the Spurs in San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, turning pensive crowds euphoric at watch parties outside the team's Madison Square Garden arena and in parks and streets across the city.
